DISCOVERY 302, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING: PLAINTIFF MARK WILSON'S MOTION TO COMPEL PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS REQUESTED UNDER DEMAND FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS, SET ONE NO. 44; REQUEST FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS IS PRO PER PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO COMPEL PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS PURSUANT TO A DEMAND FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS, SET ONE, NO. 44, ("NO. 44"), AND REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS, IS 1) PARTIALLY GRANTED TO THE EXTENT OF REQUIRING DEFENDANT DET NORSK VERITAS (U.S.A.) INC. ("DET NORSK") TO PRODUCE THE DOCUMENT REQUESTED IN NO. 44, " A COPY OF THE INTERNAL AUDIT CONDUCTED AT ONE BUSH IN DECEMBER OF 2011 BY LUC LARMUSEAU", IN A REDACTED FORMAT SO THAT THE LANGUAGE THEREIN WITH RESPECT TO PRAISING PLAINTIFF'S WORK AT DET NORSK, SUGGESTING THAT IT BE "A MODEL", AND PRAISING PLAINTIFF'S "ENERGY", AND FOR HAVING "SECURED PROJECTS", AS SUMMARIZED IN PLAINTIFF'S MOVING PAPERS, REMAINS IN THE REDACTED DOCUMENT; AND 2) DENIED INSOFAR AS IT SEEKS MONETARY SANCTIONS AGAINST DEFENDANTS. THIS RULING WILL PROVIDE PLAINTIFF WITH WHAT HE APPEARS TO BELIEVE IS IMPORTANT TO HIS CASE IN WHAT MAY BE IN THE INTERNAL AUDIT REPORT, WHILE AT THE SAME TIME PROTECTING OTHER INFORMATION IN THE REPORT FROM DISCOVERY BY PLAINTIFF THAT DOES NOT APPEAR TO BE RELEVANT TO HIS CASE, AND POSSIBLY COVERED BY THE ATTORNEY CLIENT PRIVILEGE, ALTHOUGH IT IS NOT CLEAR HOW THE PRIVILEGE MIGHT BE PRESENT WITH RESPECT TO THE REPORT. PROPOSED ORDER SUBMITTED AND TO BE SIGNED BY THE JUDGE PRO TEM. JUDGE PRO TEM: ADRIAN COLLEY, CLERK: JACQUELINE ALAMEDA, NOT REPORTED. =(302/JPT)

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT/CROSS-COMPLAINANT DET NORSKE VERITAS (U.S.A.) INC. AND DET NORSKE VERITAS' SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS GRANTED. DEFENDANTS HAVE MET THEIR INITIAL BURDEN TO DEMONSTRATE THAT THE EIGHTH CAUSE OF ACTION OF PLAINTIFF'S SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT ARISES FROM DEFENDANT'S CONSTITUTIONALLY-PROTECTED FREE SPEECH OR PETITION ACTIVITY. (EQUILON ENTERPRISES, LLC V. CONSUMER CAUSE, INC. (2002( 29 CAL.4TH 53, 61.) HERE, PLAINTIFF ALLEGES, INTER ALIA, THAT DEFENDANTS NEGLIGENTLY SUPERVISED AND RETAINED DEFENDANT RESCALVO BY PERMITTING HIM TO FILE A CROSS-COMPLAINT IN THE INSTANT ACTION. (SAC, 145.) PLAINTIFF HAS NOT MET HIS SHIFTING BURDEN TO DEMONSTRATE A PROBABILITY OF PREVAILING ON THE MERITS. IN FACT, PLAINTIFF HAS DELETED THESE ALLEGATIONS IN HIS THIRD AMENDED COMPLAINT. HOWEVER, "PLAINTIFF CANNOT AVOID AN ANTI-SLAPP MOTION BY AMENDING THE COMPLAINT PRIOR TO THE HEARING ON THE MOTION." (WEIL & BROWN, 7:1121. SEE SYLMAR AIR CONDITIONING V. PUEBLO CONTRACTING SERVICES, INC. (2004) 122 CAL.APP.4TH 1049, 1054.) THE COURT DOES NOT CONSIDER THE EVIDENCE PROFFERED FOR THE FIRST TIME IN REPLY. THE COURT WILL SIGN THE PROPOSED ORDER THIS AFTERNOON SUBMITTED BY THE PLAINTIFF IF NO OBJECTION IS RECEIVED BY THE COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST H. Off Calendar May-26-2015 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Mark Wilson, of Emmett Law, Tel. 415.500.1678, for plaintiffs;
Ryan L. Eddings, of Littler Mendelson, P.C., Tel. 559.244.7500, for defendants.

Ruling: A R G U E D. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: PLAINTIFF MARK WILSON'S Motion For New Trial/Vacate Order [CCP 657] is denied. DiGiacinto v. Ameriko-Omserv Corp. (1997) 59 Cal.App.4th 629, does not apply to this case because defendant prescribed a particular mode of acceptance and plaintiff did not accept the addendum in that manner. Defendant shifted the burden of production. Defendant's UMF 20 presented evidence showing that plaintiff refused to sign the addendum. (Rescalvo depo., pg. 123). In opposition, plaintiff failed to show proper acceptance and admitted that the addendum was kept at his desk. (Plaintiff's depo, pgs. 190 and 192). The court rejects all the cited grounds for a new trial. Order signed in open court. Judge Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk Felicia Green; Court Reporter Melanie Gheno, CSR No. 7489; Dept. 302. (302/EHG)

Off Calendar Dec-19-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Plaintiff MARK EMMETT WILSON, Tel No. (202) 262-1991 appearing in pro per;
Atty. RYAN L. EDDINGS of Littler Mendelson, P.C., Tel No. (559) 244-7500 appearing for the defendants.

RULING - A R G U E D - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Defendant Miguel Rescalvo, Det Norske Veritas As, Det Norske Veritas (U.S.A.) Inc. Motion For Summary Judgment is GRANTED. Defendants have shown Plaintiff was not entitled to commission payments after signing the Offer Letter. (Defendants' Statement of Undisputed Material Facts [DSUMF] #8.) Plaintiff has offered no evidence that rebuts that showing. Absent an allegation that the Offer Letter was unconscionable, Plaintiff's testimony as to Defendants' assurances is barred by the parole evidence rule. Also, the e-mail relied on by Plaintiff as establishing an agreement to continue commission payments does not clearly or persuasively indicate that the commissions referred to were due to or resulted from Plaintiff's efforts. (Plaintiff's Statement of Disputed Material Facts [PSDMF] #13.) As to the Addendum, Defendants' evidence establishes Plaintiff did not legally accept the offer therein, and therefore neither party is bound by its terms. As a condition of acceptance, Plaintiff was required to sign and return the Addendum to the Director of Operations and Human Resources, yet Plaintiff testified that he signed the Addendum and kept it at his desk. (PSDMF #22, 23.) Additionally, there is no reason to believe or interpret this requirement as merely a suggested method of acceptance. (C.f. Devencenzi v. Donkonics (1959) 170 Cal.App. 2d 513.) Plaintiff has also failed to establish that a triable issue of material fact exists as to intent to defraud. Rather, Plaintiff has produced evidence establishing at most the failure to perform a promise. (See Tenzer v. Superscope, Inc. (1985) 39 Cal.3d 18, 30-31.) Further, the evidence relied on by Plaintiff to circumstantially prove fraudulent intent is misconstrued and exaggerated, and does not establish insolvency, hasty repudiation, or continued assurances. (PSDMF #25.) In fact, Plaintiff's own evidence establishes that by "additional commissions," the Addendum was referring to commissions in addition to Plaintiff's salary. (PSDMF #25.) Moreover, because there are no triable issues as to whether Plaintiff was owed any commissions, Plaintiff as a matter of law will be unable to prove that he was terminated in violation of public policy. Next, Defendants have asserted and Plaintiff does not dispute that the allegedly defamatory statements are presumptively privileged, and Plaintiff has failed to show that the statements were made with malice. Defendant Rescalvo's testimony was corroborated by another employee, which provides a reasonable ground for belief in Rescalvo's statements. (DSUMF #338-40.) As to misclassification, Plaintiff has failed to identify any recoverable damages pursuant to Labor Code 227.3, or identify any other statutory provision providing for recovery of 401(k) and "other benefits." As to the breach of contract claim, Plaintiff relies on execution of the Addendum for the implied-in-fact agreement not to terminate Plaintiff without cause. However, there is no evidence that the Addendum was legally executed, and therefore neither party is bound by its term, express or implied. Finally, because Plaintiff is unable to prove any other cause of action, Defendants are entitled to judgment on the unfair business practices claim. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Sheryl L. Sawyer, CSR #5976 (302/EHG)

DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-24-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANTS MIGUEL RESCALVO, DET NORSKE VERITAS AND DET NORSKE VERITAS (U.S.A.) INC.'S DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT IS Overruled in part and sustained in part, with 20 days leave to amend. The Court did not consider any extrinsic evidence in ruling on this motion, such as the Declaration of Mark Emmett Wilson and the attached exhibits. (Blank v. Kirwan (1985) 39 Cal.3d 311, "a demurrer can be used only to challenge defects that appear on the face of the pleading under attack or from matters outside the pleading that are judicially noticeable.") As to the demurrer on uncertainty grounds, the Third Amended Complaint adequately pleads that Plaintiff was employed by both corporate entities and makes his allegations against both of them. (See, e.g. TAC, 1, 64, 73, 87, 92, 103, 109, 115; see also Khoury v. Maly's of Calif., Inc. (1993) 14 Cal.App.4th 612, 616, holding that a demurrer for uncertainty is a disfavored ground and should be overruled if facts are presumptively with defendant's knowledge.) As to the demurrer to the second cause of action for fraud, the cause of action is properly pled. (See Order Overruling Defendants' Demurrer to Plaintiff's First Amended Complaint dated May 14, 2013.) As to the demurrer to the sixth cause of action for employee misclassification, it is not preempted because the cause of action appears to seek "other related employment benefits" to remedy violations of legal duties independent of ERISA. (See TAC 113; Marin General Hospital v. Modesto & Empire Traction Co. (2009) 581 F.3d 941, 947.) As to the demurrer to the eighth cause of action for intentional interference with prospective economic advantage, the demurrer is sustained with 20 days leave to amend. See Shepppard v. Freeman (1998) 67 Cal. App. 4th 339, 349 ("employees, regardless of their scope of employment or personal motives, cannot be individually liable for their acts or words relating to personnel actions unless such liability arises from statute") ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =302/MJM

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Responses To Judicial Form Interrogatories Disc-001 & Disc-002 (Ccp 2030.300(A)(3); And Request For Monetary Sanctions (Ccp 2023.010(E) And 2023.030(A)
Off Calendar Dec-06-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Mark Wilson, Esq., Tel. 202.262.1961, plaintiff and moving party appearing on his own behalf;
No other appearance.

RULING: The party stipulated to the Pro Tem Judge. The Court adopted its uncontested tentative ruling on PLAINTIFF MARK WILSON's Motion To Compel Responses To Judicial Form Interrogatories Disc-001 & Disc-002 (CCP 2030.300(A)(3); And Request For Monetary Sanctions (CCP 2023.010(E) And 2023.030(A)). Plaintiff has brought employment-related and defamation claims against his former employer and its parent company, Det Norske Veritas AS. On the selected record before the court, an employee of the parent company, which is the relevant defendant for this motion, appears to have been involved in a managerial capacity over plaintiff's employment. Plaintiff moves to compel responses to general form interrogatories and employment form interrogatories. Plaintiff's motion to compel is granted in part and denied in part. Det Norske Veritas AS is a party to the litigation, having been stipulated in by the parties. Plaintiff has made a sufficient showing that Defendant Det Norske Veritas AS was involved, through its employee Stein, in plaintiff's hiring, compensation and termination as to warrant discovery responses from Defendant. Although Defendant may prevail on its pending demurrer, discovery responses are not the proper vehicle to argue for dismissal from the action. Defendant's argument that a different defendant has provided responsive information is not persuasive. Plaintiff is entitled to any separate knowledge or information within the possession of each defendant, both because that information may be different, but also because it may be relevant to later motion practice and/or trial. Defendant also does not adequately respond to plaintiff's authority regarding what constitutes a proper privilege objection. Accordingly, defendant is ordered to fully respond to both sets of form interrogatories, with the exception of Employment form interrogatories 201.5-201.7, which the Court finds not relevant to this matter, within 10 days of entry of the Court's order. To the extent defendant intends to rely on a privilege objection in its supplemental responses, it must fully comply with the rules relating to such objections, including specifying the privilege on which it relies and providing sufficient information to plaintiff to allow plaintiff to evaluate the propriety of the asserted privilege. Order signed in open court. Judge Pro Tem: Geoffrey Howard; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Not reported.

MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-06-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - MARK WILSON, TEL. NO. (202) 262-1961, PLAINTIFF APPEARING IN PRO PER;
ATTY. PATRICK BYRNE OF THE LITTLER LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. (559) 244-7500 EXT. 7533 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT/CROSS-COMPLAINANT DET NORSKE VERITAS (U.S.A.) INC. and DET NORSKE VERITAS' SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT is Granted. Defendants have met their initial burden to demonstrate that the eighth cause of action of Plaintiff's Second Amended Complaint arises from Defendant's constitutionally-protected free speech or petition activity. (Equilon Enterprises, LLC v. Consumer Cause, Inc. (2002( 29 Cal.4th 53, 61.) Here, Plaintiff alleges, inter alia, that Defendants negligently supervised and retained Defendant Rescalvo by permitting him to file a cross-complaint in the instant action. (SAC, 145.) Plaintiff has not met his shifting burden to demonstrate a probability of prevailing on the merits. In fact, Plaintiff has deleted these allegations in his Third Amended Complaint. However, "Plaintiff cannot avoid an anti-SLAPP motion by amending the complaint prior to the hearing on the motion." (Weil & Brown, 7:1121. See Sylmar Air Conditioning v. Pueblo Contracting Services, Inc. (2004) 122 Cal.App.4th 1049, 1054.) The Court does not consider the evidence proffered for the first time in reply. The court will sign the proposed order this afternoon submitted by the Plaintiff if no objection is received by the court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =302/EHG
